title: Shell Script for Beginners (Shell Script para Iniciantes)
venue: Festival Latino-americano de Instalação de Software Livre (FLISoL)
description: This tutorial was intended to show a little about shell script and teach about the power of this language in automating tasks in bash. Some basic concepts of the language were covered, such as syntax and the creation of small scripts to make life easier for new Linux users.

title: Introduction to Data Science with Python (Introdução ao Data Science com Python)
description: This tutorial was intended to show about how Python can be used for Data Science.

title: AlphaB3 - Expert Advisor using Artificial Neural Networks and Genetic Algorithms to Predict Stock Market Trends
venue: XVIII Escola Regional de Computação Bahia - Alagoas - Sergipe
description: Conference presentation about the AlphaB3 project.

title: Computer Vision with OpenCV (Visão Computacional com OpenCV)
venue: I Encontro de Engenharia e Tecnologia do IFBA
description: Introduction to Computer Vision and Image Processing with OpenCV.

title: kpmcore implementations and goals for the future
description: In the last two years, kpmcore library has received a lot of new patches and updates that improved the functionalities of the library. This library is mainly focused on providing partitioning processes and disk operations. As a Season of KDE student, I have contributed to the improvement of SMART support and helped in the implementation of the KAuth support to improve the authentication routines. During Google Summer of Code, my goal was focused on finishing LVM Volume Group support and start implementing MDRAID. The purpose of this presentation is to explain some of my contributions and talk about future goals for the library.

title: Computer Vision in Mobile Devices with QtQuick and OpenCV
description: The OpenCV library is one of the most referenced libraries in the area of Computer Vision, being widely used in the fields of Image and Video Processing, Object Recognition and Robotics. It is developed in C ++, being open source and multiplatform. Because it has complete portability for mobile, it is possible to use OpenCV for Android and iOS applications. The idea of this talk is to explore the integration of mobile applications made in QML and C ++ together with OpenCV. Some applications will be demonstrated, showing the compatibility between structures inherent to Qt and OpenCV (e.g. Mat and QImage), in addition to demonstrating the integration between GUIs in QML and processing with OpenCV.

title: Data Analysis with Qt
description: The idea of this talk is to present and discuss scientific applications for data analysis developed using C++ and Qt. A set of toolkit structures will be presented that are aimed at the visualization, analysis and manipulation of different types of data, focusing mainly on datasets related to big data. In addition, some details regarding the implementation of marK will be demonstrated: a tool for data manipulation and construction of datasets for machine learning that has been developed by the KDE Community.
